assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response mentions increasing maxSurge to 10 nodes but doesn't explain GKE's hard limit of ~20 nodes upgrade parallelism

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: No calculation or mathematical explanation is provided showing why 3,000 nodes can't be upgraded in 8 hours given the parallelism constraints

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response recommends manual staged upgrades across weeks (Week 1: CPU pools, Week 2: GPU pools)

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response explicitly shows upgrading CPU pools first in Week 1, then GPU pools in Week 2, and mentions prioritizing by workload type

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: While the response mentions splitting GPU pools into different time windows, it doesn't specifically address scheduling around ML training/inference workload gaps

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response recommends extending maintenance windows to 16 hours and shows splitting pools across different time windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: No mention of cluster autoscaler limitations or how it interacts with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: No mention of GKE node limits or the increased limits for AI workloads in newer versions
